document.summary: The World Factbook, a prominent intelligence publication by the CIA, has been discontinued. It transitioned from a classified document to an unclassified electronic resource over several decades, serving as a comprehensive reference for global information. Launched in 1962, it evolved significantly, becoming accessible online in 1997 and attracting millions of users annually. The publication was valued by a diverse audience, including researchers and travelers, and featured a collection of copyright-free photographs.
key_takeaways: The World Factbook was launched in 1962 as a classified publication.
It became unclassified in 1971 and was renamed in 1981.
The digital version was introduced in 1997 on CIA.gov.
It served millions of users, including researchers and travelers.
The publication included over 5,000 copyright-free photographs.
The World Factbook has now been sunsetted.
document.why_it_matters: The World Factbook was a vital resource for understanding global demographics, geography, and political structures, making it significant for researchers, educators, and the general public interested in international affairs.

timelines.milestone: Launch of The National Basic Intelligence Factbook
timelines.evidence_text: The original classified publication, titled The National Basic Intelligence Factbook, launched in 1962.
The first unclassified companion version was issued in 1971.
A decade later it was renamed The World Factbook.
In 1997, The World Factbook went digital and debuted to a worldwide audience on CIA.gov.
